S02.meta: // SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.
blocks: · Name the four policy modes for student AI use and match each to assignment purpose.
· Write student-facing policy language that is specific enough to be teachable.
· Design at least two assignment patterns that make productive AI boundaries visible and learnable.
· Draft a disclosure format that students can use without anxiety.
· Explain to a student why a boundary exists in terms of their own learning.
data.section: WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

data.title: WHY BANNING USUALLY FAILS.
blocks: They rely on detection that does not work reliably.
They do not teach anything.
They do not engage the real question.

S04.meta: // SLIDE 04 — CORE CONCEPT: FOUR POLICY MODE

data.title: CORE CONCEPT: FOUR POLICY MODES
blocks: Mode 1 — Prohibited
Mode 2 — Permitted with Disclosure
Mode 3 — Required for Process
Mode 4 — Reflective Use

S05.meta: // SLIDE 05 — WRITING POLICY LANGUAGE THAT W

data.title: WRITING POLICY LANGUAGE THAT WORKS.
blocks: · What is prohibited (submitting AI-generated text as their own)
· What is permitted (brainstorming, background research, grammar check)
· What is required when they do use AI (a brief note)

data.title: WORKED WALKTHROUGH: REDESIGNING THE VULNERABLE ASSIGNMENT
blocks: The composition instructor from the opening scene taught a research-based argument essay. Her students were supposed to
Worked Walkthrough: Redesigning the Vuln — She did not need to ban AI. She needed to redesign the work so that AI assistance is visible, bounded, and insufficient

S07.meta: // SLIDE 07 — THE HUMAN GATE: EXPLAINING THE

data.title: THE HUMAN GATE: EXPLAINING THE BOUNDARY TO STUDENTS
blocks: Students deserve to know why the boundary is where it is. "Because I said so" or "because it's cheating" are not answers
The Human Gate: Explaining the Boundary — "The reason you can't use AI to write this essay is not that I can detect it. I can't, reliably. The reason is that the

data.title: SAMPLE DISCLOSURE FORMAT.
blocks: Give students a simple format and they will use it. A disclosure requirement that is vague or burdensome will be ignored
Sample Disclosure Format — AI Use Disclosure Assignment: Submitted by: I used the following AI tools in completing this assignment: - Tool: - Stage

data.title: EQUITY AND ACCESS
blocks: Any AI policy must reckon with the fact that students do not have equal access to AI tools or equal background in using
Equity and Access — Two principles follow from this.

data.title: COMMON MISTAKES
blocks: Mistake 1: Writing a policy, not a pedagogy.
Mistake 2: Using the same mode for every assignment.
Mistake 3: Relying on detection.
Mistake 4: Treating disclosure as punishment.
Mistake 5: Ignoring the equity dimension.
